stories.headline: Alberta's government is using Claude to hunt for security holes
stories.blurb: The Government of Alberta deployed Claude across its systems to identify and patch cybersecurity vulnerabilities. It's one of the first large-scale government uses of an AI assistant for active security scanning and remediation work.
stories.why_it_matters: Shows AI moving from enterprise pilots to critical infrastructure defense
stories.original_headline: Government of Alberta uses Claude to find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities across government systems

stories.headline: You can now adjust how fast Siri talks and how much personality it has
stories.blurb: iOS 27 beta lets you customize Siri's speaking pace and expressivity—making the assistant sound more natural or neutral depending on your preference. It's part of Apple's rebuild of Siri around generative AI, which aims to make interactions feel less robotic and more personal.
stories.why_it_matters: Apple is finally letting you shape how Siri sounds, not just what it says.
stories.original_headline: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta

stories.headline: NVIDIA and Hugging Face just made training robots way cheaper
stories.blurb: The two companies are pooling robot foundation models, datasets, and simulation tools into LeRobot, Hugging Face's open robotics platform. Developers can now train and test robot behaviors without buying expensive hardware first—using NVIDIA's Isaac sim and pre-trained models that already understand how physical objects work.
stories.why_it_matters: Robotics development just got the same open-source boost that made LLMs explode.
stories.original_headline: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Bring New Models and Frameworks to LeRobot for the Open Robotics Community

stories.headline: DeepSeek is reportedly building its own AI chips
stories.blurb: The Chinese startup that shocked the industry by training frontier models for a fraction of typical costs is now developing custom AI chips, according to Reuters. If successful, DeepSeek could control its entire stack — from silicon to software — potentially widening its cost advantage even further.
stories.why_it_matters: Cheap models plus cheap chips could reshape who can afford to compete in AI
stories.original_headline: Reuters: DeepSeek is developing its own AI chips

try_this_prompt.title: The meeting subtraction
try_this_prompt.prompt: I have a weekly meeting I'll describe below. Don't tell me how to make it better — assume it's already well-run. Instead: tell me what would break if we cancelled it for a month, what we'd lose nothing by moving to async, and which two people actually need to be there.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you accept that calendar invite for the next six months.
